content.text:
This view clearly shows the connections of atom **C:1**. I can see three bonds radiating from C:1. Let me confirm from this angle:

Based on my visual inspection, atom **C:1** (Carbon) has the following covalently bonded neighbour atoms:

| Atomic Index | Element |
|---|---|
| 0 | O (Oxygen) |
| 2 | N (Nitrogen) |
| 27 | S (Sulfur) |

**Summary:**
- **Atom 0 (O)** — bonded directly below C:1
- **Atom 2 (N)** — bonded above C:1
- **Atom 27 (S)** — bonded to the right of C:1

**geomconf_index:** **8385**

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 1, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
